papers.title: Aerial Multi-Functional RIS in Fluid Antennas-Aided Full-Duplex Networks: A Self-Optimized Hybrid Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
papers.abstract: To address high data traffic demands of sixth-generation (6G) networks, this paper proposes a novel architecture that integrates autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) and multi-functional reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (MF-RISs) as AM-RIS in fluid antenna (FA)-assisted full-duplex (FD) networks. The AM-RIS provides hybrid functionalities, including signal reflection, amplification, and energy harvesting (EH), potentially improving both signal coverage and sustainability. Meanwhile, FA facilitates fine-grained spatial adaptability at FD-enabled base station (BS), which complements residual self-interference (SI) suppression. We aim at maximizing the overall energy efficiency (EE) by jointly optimizing transmit DL beamforming at BS, UL user power, configuration of AM-RIS, and positions of the FA and AM-RIS. Owing to the hybrid continuous-discrete parameters and high dimensionality of the intractable problem, we have conceived a self-optimized multi-agent hybrid deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework (SOHRL), which integrates multi-agent deep Q-networks (DQN) and multi-agent proximal policy optimization (PPO), respectively handling discrete and continuous actions. To enhance self-adaptability, an attention-driven state representation and meta-level hyperparameter optimization are incorporated, enabling multi-agents to autonomously adjust learning hyperparameters. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed AM-RIS-enabled FA-aided FD networks empowered by SOHRL algorithm. The results reveal that SOHRL outperforms benchmarks of the case without attention mechanism and conventional hybrid/multi-agent/standalone DRL. Moreover, AM-RIS in FD achieves the highest EE compared to half-duplex, conventional rigid antenna arrays, partial EH, and conventional RIS without amplification, highlighting its potential as a compelling solution for EE-aware wireless networks.
papers.summary_cn: 针对6G网络高数据流量需求，本文提出融合自主飞行器与多功能可重构智能表面（AM-RIS）的新架构，应用于流体天线辅助全双工网络。AM-RIS具备反射、放大和能量收集功能。通过联合优化波束成形、功率、AM-RIS配置及位置，采用混合深度强化学习框架（SOHRL）最大化能效。仿真验证方案优越性。

strengths: Novelous system architecture: integrating aerial MF-RIS with FA-assisted FD networks addresses a real 6G research gap
Well-motivated problem: EE maximization under complex hybrid discrete-continuous action space is practically relevant
Comprehensive joint optimization: beamforming, power, RIS config, and positioning are all considered together
Hybrid DRL framework (DQN+PPO) is architecturally sound for handling heterogeneous action spaces
Attention-driven state representation and meta-hyperparameter optimization add adaptive self-improvement capability
Clear and well-written abstract with logical structure and good English
Addresses sustainability via energy harvesting and EE awareness
limitations: SIMULATION ONLY evaluation — no real datasets, no real-world validation; this is a critical data quality weakness
No mathematical formulation visible in abstract — cannot assess theoretical rigor or optimality guarantees
Novelty is largely architectural combination of known concepts (DRL hybrids exist; aerial RIS and FA are known topics) rather than a fundamentally new formulation
Full-duplex BS, aerial platforms (drones), and MF-RIS are all immature/deployment-distant technologies — practical impact is speculative
Meta-hyperparameter optimization adds complexity; may be hard to reproduce and tune in practice
The many design knobs (AM-RIS hybrid functions, FA positions, EH) may make the problem over-parameterized and hard to generalize
Baseline comparisons (no attention, standalone DRL variants) are expected but the specific performance claims lack statistical rigor without variance/confidence intervals in abstract
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: All evaluation is simulation-based with synthetic data; no real-world datasets or measurements are used. This is a significant limitation — the paper cannot demonstrate real-world viability, measurement-based accuracy, or deployment readiness. Simulation-only results are common in early 6G research but weaken claims of practical impact.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper presents a well-motivated, architecturally novel system combining AM-RIS, fluid antennas, and full-duplex with a sophisticated hybrid DRL framework. However, evaluation is purely simulation-based with no real datasets, and the abstract provides insufficient mathematical detail to assess theoretical rigor. The novelty is primarily in the system-level integration rather than fundamental algorithmic or theoretical breakthroughs. Overall score 43 reflects moderate novelty, acceptable rigor, weak data quality, good presentation, and meaningful but premature practical impact.

papers.title: Joint Trajectory and Resource Optimization for Aerial RIS-assisted Integrated TNT Networks
papers.authors: Vangara Saiprudhvi, Sandeep Singh, Keshav Singh, Hariharan Subramaniyam, Chih-Peng Li
papers.affiliations: Institute | National University | Department School | School Institute
papers.abstract: Integrated terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks (ITNTNs) are regarded as a key architectural paradigm for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems. This paper investigates a dual-aerial reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted ITNTN, where a terrestrial base station (TBS) and a satellite (SAT) jointly serve terrestrial and satellite users with the aid of an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-mounted RIS and a high-altitude platform (HAP)-mounted RIS. We formulate an average sum-rate maximization problem by jointly optimizing the TBS and SAT precoders, the RIS phase shift matrices, and the three-dimensional trajectories of the UAV and the HAP, subject to transmit power, unit-modulus, and mobility constraints. The resulting optimization problem is highly non-convex due to the strong coupling among the transmit precoders, RIS phase shifts, and aerial platform mobility. To efficiently address this challenge, we propose a block coordinate descent (BCD) framework that integrates weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) optimization for precoder design, a manifold-based Riemannian conjugate gradient (RCG) method for RIS phase-shift optimization, and successive convex approximation (SCA) for trajectory optimization. The proposed algorithm is shown to converge to a stationary point. The simulation results show that the proposed joint design achieves an approximately $7.05 \%$ higher average sum-rate compared to the random RIS scheme, highlighting the effectiveness of dual-aerial RIS deployment and joint communication-mobility optimization in ITNTNs.
papers.summary_cn: 本文研究双空中RIS辅助的天地一体化网络（ITNTN），地面基站与卫星在UAV-RIS和HAP-RIS协同下服务用户。构建平均和速率最大化问题，联合优化预编码器、RIS相移及飞行平台轨迹。提出块坐标下降框架，结合WMMSE、黎曼共轭梯度和逐次凸逼近方法。仿真显示联合设计相比随机RIS提升约7.05%。

strengths: Dual-aerial RIS (UAV + HAP) in an integrated terrestrial/non-terrestrial network is a reasonably novel system model combining two aerial tiers with satellite and terrestrial infrastructure
Well-structured optimization formulation with proper constraints (power, unit-modulus, mobility)
BCD framework with WMMSE + RCG + SCA is a methodical and sound decomposition; convergence to a stationary point is claimed
Clear and organized abstract; problem statement and methodology are well-articulated
limitations: The 7.05% gain over random RIS phase shifts is a very weak baseline — random is essentially a straw-man; comparison against optimized single-aerial RIS, fixed-position RIS, or state-of-the-art alternating optimization schemes is needed
Purely simulation-based evaluation with no real-world or even semi-empirical dataset; no channel measurement validation or trace-driven simulation
BCD + WMMSE + RCG + SCA is a highly standard recipe in the aerial RIS literature; the methodological contribution is incremental rather than innovative
HAP-mounted RIS is far from practical deployment; the system model is largely theoretical with unclear path to real-world implementation
Abstract does not discuss computational complexity, convergence speed, or scalability with more users/RIS elements — critical for aerial platforms with limited compute
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No real-world data or channel measurements used. Evaluation is entirely simulation-based. The only reported quantitative result (7.05% over random RIS) uses an uncompetitive baseline, making it difficult to assess the true merit of the joint optimization. Missing: comparison against state-of-the-art optimized RIS baselines, convergence analysis, and complexity results.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This paper follows a well-trodden path in the aerial RIS optimization literature: formulate a non-convex sum-rate problem, apply BCD decomposition with standard sub-problem solvers (WMMSE, RCG, SCA), and report modest gains over trivial baselines. The dual-aerial (UAV+HAP) system model adds some novelty, but the methodology is incremental and the experimental validation is weak. The 7.05% gain over random RIS is unconvincing. Likely a mid-tier journal or conference paper.

papers.title: Joint Trajectory and Resource Optimization for Dual-aerial ARIS-assisted NOMA-TNT Networks
papers.authors: Vangara Saiprudhvi, Keshav Singh, Hariharan Subramaniyam, Chih-Peng Li
papers.affiliations: Institute | National University | Department School | School Institute
papers.abstract: Integrated terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks (ITNTNs) are envisioned as a key paradigm for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems, enabling seamless global connectivity. In this paper, we investigate a dual-aerial active reconfigurable intelligent surface (ARIS)-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-based ITNTN, where a terrestrial base station (TBS) and a satellite (SAT) simultaneously serve terrestrial and satellite users with the aid of a UAV-mounted ARIS and a HAP-mounted ARIS. Users are multiplexed via power-domain NOMA with a predefined SIC decoding order. We formulate an average sum-rate maximization problem by jointly optimizing transmit beamforming, ARIS coefficients, and the 3D trajectories of the UAV and HAP, subject to power, unit-modulus, ARIS power, and mobility constraints. The problem is highly non-convex due to coupled variables, nonlinear SINR expressions, ARIS amplification, and trajectory-dependent channels. To address this, a block coordinate descent (BCD)-based framework is proposed. Specifically, beamforming is optimized via WMMSE, ARIS phase shifts via a manifold-based RCG method, amplification factors via SCA, and trajectories via first-order approximations. The proposed algorithm is guaranteed to converge to a stationary point. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves significant performance gains over benchmark schemes. In particular, it provides an average sum-rate improvement of approximately $8.44\%$ over passive RIS under given power constraints, highlighting the benefits of dual-aerial ARIS and joint communication-mobility optimization.
papers.summary_cn: 本文研究双空中有源RIS（ARIS）辅助的NOMA天地一体化网络，地面基站与卫星在UAV-ARIS和HAP-ARIS协同下服务用户。构建平均和速率最大化问题，联合优化波束成形、ARIS系数及轨迹。提出块坐标下降框架，采用WMMSE、黎曼共轭梯度和逐次凸逼近方法。仿真表明相比无源RIS提升约8.44%。

strengths: Combines multiple trending 6G topics (NOMA, ARIS, ITNTN, dual-aerial platforms) into a unified framework, which is ambitious in scope.
Proposes a BCD-based framework with dedicated sub-problem solvers (WMMSE, manifold-based RCG, SCA), which is methodologically sound for this class of problems.
Considers practical constraints including unit-modulus, ARIS power budgets, and 3D mobility constraints for both UAV and HAP.
limitations: The novelty is incremental: each individual component (NOMA, RIS optimization, UAV trajectory design, BCD) is well-established in existing literature; combining them is a packaging exercise rather than a methodological breakthrough.

The claimed 8.44% improvement over passive RIS is underwhelming — active RIS providing only single-digit percentage gains raises questions about whether the additional hardware complexity (amplifiers on aerial platforms) is justified.

No mention of real-world datasets, channel measurements, or experimental validation — appears to rely entirely on Monte Carlo simulations with standard channel models (e.g., Rayleigh/Rician), which limits practical credibility.
The problem formulation (sum-rate maximization with BCD) follows a highly templated structure seen in hundreds of RIS/UAV optimization papers, reducing the perceived contribution.

NOMA decoding with predefined SIC order in a dual-aerial ITNTN introduces significant fairness and practicality concerns that are not addressed (e.g., near-far effects across terrestrial-satellite users).
Satellite trajectory optimization is unrealistic — LEO/MEO satellite orbits are largely deterministic, not freely optimizable as implied.
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No indication of real-world data or experimental validation. Likely relies on standard Monte Carlo simulations with Rayleigh/Rician fading models. Benchmark schemes are probably conventional baselines (passive RIS, no-RIS, orthogonal multiple access). The 8.44% improvement figure suggests modest gains. Without large-scale real-channel data or hardware prototype results, the evaluation is simulation-only and of limited practical说服力.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper is a systematic but incremental contribution in a crowded research area. The methodology (BCD+WMMSE+SCA+manifold) is standard and well-understood. The combination of dual-aerial ARIS with NOMA-TNTN is moderately novel but lacks a compelling practical motivation or experimental evidence. The 8.44% gain is a weak selling point for active RIS with significantly higher hardware cost. Likely suitable for a mid-tier IEEE journal but not a top venue (IEEE JSAC, TWC, or TCOM might accept with revisions; IEEE Access/TVT is more likely).

papers.title: ESN-DAGMM: A Lightweight Framework for Unsupervised Time-Series Data Monitoring in 5G O-RAN Networks
papers.authors: Andrew J Chen, Raymond Zhao, Lingjia Liu
papers.abstract: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) is an important 5G network architecture enabling flexible communication with adaptive strategies for different verticals. However, testing for O-RAN deployments involve massive volumes of time-series data (e.g., key performance indicators), creating critical challenges for scalable, unsupervised monitoring without labels or high computational overhead. To address this, we present ESN-DAGMM, a lightweight adaptation of the Deep Autoencoding Gaussian Mixture Model (DAGMM) framework for time series analysis. Our model utilizes an Echo State Network (ESN) to efficiently model temporal dependencies, proving effective in O-RAN networks where training samples are highly limited. Combined with DAGMM's integratation of dimensionality reduction and density estimation, we present a scalable framework for unsupervised monitoring of high volume network telemetry. When trained on only 10% of an O-RAN video-streaming dataset, ESN-DAGMM achieved on average 269.59% higher quality clustering than baselines under identical conditions, all while maintaining competitive reconstruction error. By extending DAGMM to capture temporal dynamics, ESN-DAGMM offers a practical solution for time-series analysis using very limited training samples, outperforming baselines and enabling operator's control over the clustering-reconstruction trade-off.
papers.summary_cn: O-RAN部署测试涉及海量时序KPI数据，无监督监控面临可扩展性和计算开销挑战。本文提出ESN-DAGMM，结合回声状态网络(ESN)建模时序依赖与DAGMM的降维和密度估计，实现轻量级无监督监控。仅用10%训练样本，ESN-DAGMM聚类质量比基线平均提升269.59%，同时保持竞争力重构误差，为有限样本下的O-RAN时序分析提供实用方案。

strengths: Timely and practical problem: unsupervised monitoring of O-RAN KPIs is a real industry need
ESN as a lightweight temporal encoder is a sensible choice for limited-sample regimes — avoids full RNN training overhead
Extending DAGMM to temporal data is a logical and underexplored direction
10% training data claim is interesting and relevant for operator cost constraints
Scalability framing (high-volume telemetry) aligns well with O-RAN's architecture goals
limitations: Critical: no information about the O-RAN dataset — real vs simulated, scale (number of UEs/samples/timesteps), collection methodology, or whether it is publicly available
269.59% 'quality clustering' improvement is unexplained — which metric (NMI/AMI/DB index)? No absolute numbers provided, making the claim unverifiable and potentially misleading
No comparison against established unsupervised baselines: Isolation Forest, LOF, One-Class SVM, LSTM-AE, or standard DAGMM variants
DAGMM compression/estimation networks not described at all — impossible to assess architectural soundness or reproduce the method
No ablation study on ESN reservoir size, spectral radius, or sparsity — critical for reproducibility
No evaluation on publicly available time-series anomaly benchmarks (Yahoo S5, SMD, SWaT) — cross-domain credibility is missing
Reconstruction error reported but not anchored to any detection threshold methodology — no F1/AUROC/AUPR numbers
'Competitive reconstruction error' is vague — does not clarify whether competitive means better, equal, or slightly worse
No statistical significance testing reported across runs
The paper reads more as a system design poster than a rigorous research contribution — theory and derivation are essentially absent
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The O-RAN video-streaming dataset is not described with sufficient detail: data source (simulation vs real deployment), scale, feature set, label availability, or public accessibility are all undisclosed. The 10% training split is mentioned but its rationale and impact on generalization are not evaluated. No external benchmark datasets are used for cross-validation, making it impossible to assess whether claims generalize beyond the authors' internal data. This is a significant red flag for a paper whose primary contribution is empirical.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The core idea of combining ESN (temporal encoder) with DAGMM (density estimation head) is a plausible and somewhat novel integration, and the O-RAN application domain is timely. However, the paper as presented reads like an early-stage work with critical gaps: no dataset description, unexplained metric, no established baselines, no architectural details, and no reproducibility path. A score of 46 reflects that the idea has merit but the execution is too incomplete for a strong assessment — more like a workshop paper than a journal/conference submission.

papers.title: Learning Low-Dimensional Representation for O-RAN Testing via Transformer-ESN
papers.authors: Jiongyu Dai, Raymond Zhao, Farhad Rezazadeh, Lizhong Zheng, Haining Wang, Lingjia Liu
papers.abstract: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architectures enhance flexibility for 6G and NextG networks. However, it also brings significant challenges in O-RAN testing with evaluating abundant, high-dimensional key performance indicators (KPIs). In this paper, we introduce a novel two-stage framework to learn temporally-aware low-dimensional representations of O-RAN testing KPIs. To be specific, stage one employs an information-theoretic H-score to train a hybrid self-attentive transformer and echo state network (ESN) reservoir, called Transformer-ESN, capturing temporal dynamics and producing task-aligned $8$-dimensional embeddings. Stage two evaluates these embeddings by training a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) predictor exclusively on them for key target KPIs such as reference signal received quality (RSRQ) and spectral efficiency. Using real-world O-RAN testbed data (video streaming with interference), our approach demonstrates a significant advantage specifically when training samples are very limited. In this scenario, the low-dimensional representations learned from the Transformer-ESN yield mean square error (MSE) reductions of up to 41.9\% for RSRQ and 29.9\% for spectral efficiency compared to predictions from the original high-dimensional data. The framework exhibits high efficiency for O-RAN testing, significantly reducing testing complexities for O-RAN systems.
papers.summary_cn: O-RAN测试需评估高维KPI，本文提出两阶段框架学习时序感知低维表征：第一阶段用信息论H-score训练Transformer-ESN混合模型，生成8维任务对齐嵌入；第二阶段用轻量MLP预测RSRQ和频谱效率等目标KPI。基于真实O-RAN测试床数据，在训练样本极度有限时，低维表征使RSRQ的MSE降低41.9%、频谱效率MSE降低29.9%，显著降低O-RAN系统测试复杂度。

strengths: Novel combination of Transformer self-attention with ESN reservoir computing for temporal dynamics capture
Uses information-theoretic H-score for training embeddings, which is a principled approach
Addresses a real O-RAN testing challenge: high-dimensional KPI evaluation complexity
Two-stage architecture (representation learning + prediction) is conceptually sound
Strong practical motivation with clear industry relevance for 6G/NextG networks
Good presentation clarity with well-structured abstract
limitations: Data details severely lacking - no scale (sample count), no data collection duration, no feature dimension specified
No baseline comparison with other representation learning methods (PCA, autoencoders, etc.)
Claim of 'real-world' testbed data lacks reproducibility details (vendor, deployment scenario)
No clear comparison against end-to-end deep learning approaches
MSE reduction percentages lack statistical significance (variance, confidence intervals)
Unclear what makes ESN essential vs. standard LSTM/GRU for temporal modeling
8-dimensional embedding choice not justified (ablation study missing)
Video streaming scenario may not generalize to other O-RAN use cases
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Claims real-world O-RAN testbed data but provides zero quantitative details: sample size, feature dimensionality, collection period, interference characteristics, and testbed setup are all omitted. Cannot verify if dataset scale supports the claims or if baselines are state-of-the-art.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Scored based on abstract alone. Strong motivation and novel architecture (Transformer-ESN) but data section is essentially empty - a real O-RAN testbed is claimed but no specifics given. Medium confidence because key technical details (why this architecture, H-score formulation, baseline methods) are missing from abstract.

papers.title: Efficient Transceiver Design for Aerial Image Transmission and Large-scale Scene Reconstruction
papers.authors: Zeyi Ren, Jialin Dong, Wei Zuo, Yikun Wang, Bingyang Cheng, Sheng Zhou, Zhisheng Niu
papers.abstract: Large-scale three-dimensional (3D) scene reconstruction in low-altitude intelligent networks (LAIN) demands highly efficient wireless image transmission. However, existing schemes struggle to balance severe pilot overhead with the transmission accuracy required to maintain reconstruction fidelity. To strike a balance between efficiency and reliability, this paper proposes a novel deep learning-based end-to-end (E2E) transceiver design that integrates 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) directly into the training process. By jointly optimizing the communication modules via the combined 3DGS rendering loss, our approach explicitly improves scene recovery quality. Furthermore, this task-driven framework enables the use of a sparse pilot scheme, significantly reducing transmission overhead while maintaining robust image recovery under low-altitude channel conditions. Extensive experiments on real-world aerial image datasets demonstrate that the proposed E2E design significantly outperforms existing baselines, delivering superior transmission performance and accurate 3D scene reconstructions.
papers.summary_cn: 低空智能网络中大规模3D场景重建需高效无线图像传输。本文提出深度学习端到端收发机设计，将3D高斯泼溅（3DGS）融入训练过程，通过渲染损失联合优化通信模块。任务驱动框架支持稀疏导频方案，在降低传输开销的同时保持重建质量。实验表明该设计显著优于现有方案。

strengths: Integration of 3DGS rendering loss directly into E2E transceiver training is a creative task-driven design that aligns communication optimization with the downstream 3D reconstruction objective
Sparse pilot scheme enabled by the task-driven framework addresses a real overhead problem in aerial communication
Use of real-world aerial image datasets rather than purely synthetic data lends credibility
Clear and well-structured abstract that articulates the problem, gap, and contribution
limitations: E2E transceiver + task-oriented communication is an established paradigm; the novelty is primarily in the specific 3DGS integration rather than a fundamentally new formulation
Abstract provides no detail on channel model assumptions, MIMO configuration, or how 'low-altitude channel conditions' are modeled — rigor is hard to assess
Dataset scale, diversity, and baseline selection are unspecified; 'extensive experiments on real-world aerial image datasets' is a common but unverifiable claim at abstract level
No mention of computational complexity or inference latency of the 3DGS-augmented transceiver, which matters for real-time aerial deployment
Practical deployment gap remains large: lab-trained E2E transceivers with DL-based channel models rarely survive real-world fading and hardware impairments
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract explicitly claims real-world aerial image datasets, which is a positive signal. However, no details on dataset scale, geographic diversity, or whether channel conditions are simulated or measured. Baseline comprehensiveness is also unclear. Without full paper verification, data quality appears adequate but not exceptional.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper sits at the intersection of task-oriented communication and 3D reconstruction, which is timely but builds on well-established E2E autoencoder paradigms. The 3DGS rendering loss as a training signal is a meaningful twist, but the core novelty is incremental. Data quality benefits from real-world images but channel modeling specifics are unknown. Practical impact is promising for UAV/surveying use cases but depends heavily on deployment robustness not demonstrated in the abstract.

papers.title: Disaggregated multi-domain interference classification for O-RAN
papers.authors: Dieter Verbruggen, Hazem Sallouha, Sofie Pollin
papers.abstract: Spectrum sharing and dynamic spectrum reuse are becoming increasingly critical in modern wireless networks to address spectrum scarcity. However, these techniques inevitably increase Cross-Technology Interference (CTI). In this context, the Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN), as a modern and disaggregated network architecture, necessitates accurate, low-latency, and computationally efficient CTI classification and mitigation to support real-time control and maintain Quality of Service (QoS). Unfortunately, existing solutions predominantly rely on high-complexity, monolithic deep learning-based solutions that, while achieving high classification accuracy, incur significant latency and computational overhead This paper exploits the O-RAN functional split to leverage multi-domain raw signal representations (time, frequency, and Channel State Information (CSI)) directly from the same data stream. Each domain is processed locally, naturally interleaving CTI within the distributed, disaggregated O-RAN architecture. This distributed strategy enables a cost-aware, multi-domain fusion architecture that balances classification accuracy with computational overhead and latency. Our proposed multi-domain distributed architecture achieves a 400 $μs$ inference latency on standard CPUs. Compared to a state-of-the-art monolithic frequency-domain classifier, this represents an average 9x reduction in latency and an 11-fold decrease in computational cost, while sacrificing only 4% in classification performance and maintaining >90% accuracy in high-interference conditions.
papers.summary_cn: 频谱共享增加跨技术干扰(CTI)，O-RAN架构需要低延迟高效率的CTI分类与缓解。现有方案依赖高复杂度单体深度学习，延迟和计算开销大。本文利用O-RAN功能切分，从同一数据流中提取时域、频域和CSI多域表征，在分布式架构中本地处理并融合，实现精度-开销平衡。所提架构在CPU上推理延迟400μs，比单体频域分类器延迟降低9倍、计算量减少11倍，仅损失4%分类精度，高干扰条件下仍保持>90%准确率。

strengths: Practical architectural alignment with O-RAN functional split — the distributed processing naturally maps to disaggregated RAN components, which is a sensible design choice
Impressive claimed latency reduction (9x) and computational cost savings (11x) while maintaining >90% accuracy, demonstrating a favorable accuracy-cost trade-off
Multi-domain fusion (time, frequency, CSI) from a single data stream is an efficient use of available information without requiring additional sensors
400μs inference on standard CPUs is practically relevant for real-time O-RAN xApp/rApp deployment, avoiding GPU dependency
limitations: Abstract mentions no specific datasets, evaluation scenarios, or interference types tested — unclear whether evaluation uses real-world RF captures or only synthetic simulations
No comparison against other distributed or lightweight approaches; the only baseline mentioned is a monolithic frequency-domain classifier, which is a relatively weak comparison point
4% accuracy sacrifice characterization lacks context — does this affect specific interference classes disproportionately? Class-level performance is critical for CTI mitigation
Cost-aware fusion mechanism is vague — no detail on how the cost model is defined or whether it adapts dynamically at runtime

Multi-domain signal extraction from the same data stream is claimed but not explained — time-frequency transforms and CSI estimation from a single stream raises questions about independence of features
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The abstract provides zero information about the evaluation dataset. No mention of real-world RF measurements, synthetic channel models (e.g., 3GPP TR 38.901), interference technology types included, SNR ranges, or dataset scale. This is a significant red flag — CTI classification work heavily depends on the diversity and realism of interference scenarios, and purely synthetic evaluation would severely limit the paper's credibility. State-of-the-art baselines are also unclear.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses a relevant O-RAN problem with a practical architectural approach and strong latency claims, but the complete absence of dataset details, limited baseline comparisons, and incremental novelty (multi-domain + distributed is not a new combination conceptually) cap the score. If the full paper reveals real-world RF datasets and comprehensive baselines, the score could rise to 60-65 range; if evaluation is simulation-only with narrow interference types, it could drop below 50.

papers.title: Aerial IRS Deployment-Aided Secure Computation Offloading Against DISCO Jamming Attacks
papers.authors: Minghui Min, Peng Zhang, Jiayang Xiao, Ruixin Yang, Shiyin Li, Huan Huang, Hongliang Zhang, Zhu Han
papers.affiliations: China Universityof Miningand Technology,Xuzhou221116,China,andalso | College | University,Singapore639798.E-mail:minmh@cumt.edu.cn | China University School | School | University,China
papers.abstract: With the rapid growth of Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC), secure and efficient computation offloading from user equipment (UEs) to edge access points (APs) is critical. However, DISCO intelligent reflective surface-based fully-passive jammers (DIRS-based FPJs) use random time-varying phase shifts to launch DISCO jamming attacks, disrupting offloading performance. This paper leverages an aerial intelligent reflective surface (AIRS) to enable secure computation offloading against DISCO jamming by jointly optimizing offloading ratios, AIRS phase shifts, and deployment. A two-timescale (2Ts) framework is proposed to address the optimization challenge caused by the distinct update frequencies of different strategies. Specifically, AIRS deployment is adjusted on a long timescale to boost antijamming capability due to the impracticality of frequent physical adjustment, while offloading ratios and phase shifts are optimized on a short timescale to adapt to DIRS-jammed dynamic channel conditions. We propose a dual-agent deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based AIRS deployment-aided secure computation offloading (DDADSO) scheme to maximize the secure offloading utility under DISCO jamming. Simulation results verify that the proposed DDADSO scheme outperforms benchmark schemes, demonstrating the effectiveness of AIRS deployment in improving offloading performance against DISCO jamming attacks.
papers.summary_cn: 针对MEC中的DISCO智能反射面干扰攻击问题，本文利用空中智能反射面（AIRS）实现安全计算卸载。提出双时间尺度框架：长时尺调整AIRS部署，短时尺优化卸载比例和相移。设计双智能体深度强化学习方案（DDADSO），联合优化策略以最大化安全卸载效用。仿真表明该方案有效抵御DISCO干扰。

strengths: Addresses a timely and relevant problem: securing MEC computation offloading against intelligent jamming (DISCO), which is an emerging threat model beyond conventional jamming.

The two-timescale (2Ts) framework is well-motivated — physically adjusting IRS deployment is inherently slow, while phase shifts and offloading ratios can adapt faster. This separation is practically sound.
Jointly optimizing three interdependent variables (offloading ratios, AIRS phase shifts, and AIRS deployment position) captures the core trade-off space of the problem.
The use of aerial IRS (AIRS) rather than fixed IRS adds deployment flexibility, which is a meaningful extension for dynamic MEC scenarios.
limitations: The problem formulation and solution methodology closely follow the now-standard IRS + DRL optimization template. Many prior works already use DRL for IRS phase shift optimization, and two-timescale frameworks are well-established in the IRS literature. The incremental contribution is mainly in the specific threat model (DISCO jamming).

Dual-agent DRL introduces significant training complexity and hyperparameter sensitivity. The abstract provides no discussion of convergence, training overhead, or stability — critical concerns for DRL-based schemes in dynamic environments.

No analytical characterization of the DISCO jamming model's impact on offloading performance. A rigorous information-theoretic or game-theoretic baseline analysis would strengthen the paper significantly.
The scalability of the proposed scheme is unclear. As the number of UEs, DIRS-based jammers, or IRS elements increases, the joint action space grows combinatorially, which may render DRL impractical.
Only 'simulation results' are mentioned — no real-world testbed validation or even system-level simulation (e.g., using ns-3 or a channel emulator).
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Purely simulation-based evaluation with no real-world datasets or system-level simulators. The DISCO jamming channel model is likely analytically simplified. No mention of dataset scale, number of evaluation scenarios, or statistical significance testing. Benchmark schemes are not specified in the abstract.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses a relevant intersection of MEC security, IRS, and adversarial jamming, but the contribution is largely incremental — combining existing IRS optimization techniques (DRL + two-timescale) with a specific jamming model. The absence of real-world validation, analytical depth, and clarity on DRL scalability limits its impact. A mid-tier conference (e.g., IEEE GLOBECOM, ICC, or VTC) is a likely venue.

papers.title: LITE: Lightweight Channel Gain Estimation with Reduced X-Haul CSI Signaling in O-RAN
papers.authors: David Goez, Marco Piazzola, Giulia Costa, Achiel Colpaert, Rodney Martinez Alonso, Esra Aycan Beyazit, Nina Slamnik-Krijestorac, Johann M. Marquez-Barja, Miguel Camelo Botero
papers.abstract: Cell-Free Massive Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (CF-MaMIMO) in Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) promises high spectral efficiency but is limited by frequent Channel State Information (CSI) exchanges, which strain fronthaul/midhaul/backhaul (X-haul) bandwidth and exceed the capabilities of existing approaches relying on uncompressed CSI or heavy predictors. To overcome these constraints, we propose LITE, a lightweight pipeline combining a 1-D convolutional Autoencoder (AE) at the O-RAN Distributed Unit (O-DU) with a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE)-enhanced Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) predictor at the Near-Real-Time RAN Intelligent Controller (Near-RT-RIC), enabling short-horizon trajectory-unaware forecasting under strict transport and processing budgets. LITE applies 50% CSI compression and an asymmetric SE-BiLSTM, reducing model complexity by 83.39% while improving accuracy by 5% relative to a baseline BiLSTM. With compression-aware training, the Lightweight Intelligent Trajectory Estimator (LITE) incurs only 6% accuracy loss versus the BiLSTM baseline, outperforming independent and end-to-end strategies. A TensorRT-optimized implementation achieves 147k Queries per Second (QPS), a 4.6x throughput gain. These results demonstrate that LITE delivers X-haul-efficient, low-latency, and deployment-ready channel-gain prediction compatible with O-RAN splits.
papers.summary_cn: O-RAN中无小区大规模MIMO受限于频繁CSI交换对X-haul带宽的压力。本文提出LITE轻量级流水线：O-DU端1-D卷积自编码器压缩CSI，Near-RT-RIC端SE增强BiLSTM预测信道增益。LITE实现50%CSI压缩，模型复杂度降低83.39%，精度提升5%；压缩感知训练仅6%精度损失，优于独立和端到端策略。TensorRT优化实现147k QPS，吞吐提升4.6倍，满足O-RAN切分下X-haul高效低延迟部署需求。

strengths: Addresses practical O-RAN deployment constraints with X-haul bandwidth optimization
Impressive efficiency gains: 83.39% complexity reduction with 5% accuracy improvement
Compression-aware training shows thoughtful integration of prediction and compression tasks
TensorRT optimization and 147k QPS demonstrates deployment readiness
Clear problem formulation for CF-MaMIMO in O-RAN architecture
limitations: No real-world CSI dataset or field trial mentioned - relies on simulation/synthetic data
6% accuracy loss after compression may be problematic for practical MIMO systems requiring high channel estimation precision
Short-horizon prediction limits applicability for proactive resource allocation
Comparison to only BiLSTM baseline is narrow - lacks comparison to state-of-the-art CSI prediction methods
Novelty is incremental: Autoencoder compression and BiLSTM prediction are established techniques
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract does not mention any real-world dataset, field measurements, or testbed experiments. Appears to rely entirely on synthetic/simulated CSI data, which significantly undermines practical validity claims for O-RAN deployment scenarios.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses an important practical problem in O-RAN deployments and achieves impressive efficiency metrics. However, the lack of real-world data validation is a critical weakness that undermines the deployment-ready claims. Novelty is moderate (combination of existing techniques). Score reflects strong engineering contribution but limited research advancement and unvalidated practical applicability.

papers.title: Aerial Booster-Cell Enabled Inter-Cell Interference Coordination for 5G NR Networks
papers.authors: Md Sharif Hossen, Vijay K. Shah, Ismail Guvenc
papers.abstract: Cellular-connected unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) operating in 5G New Radio (NR) macro networks experience severe and spatially non-uniform downlink interference. This is primarily caused by the interference from the sidelobes of downtilted base station (BS) antennas serving terrestrial users, which limits the ability of the network to provide uniform and high-quality coverage to aerial users. Supporting aerial users requires boosting the coverage of certain cells or sectors, which can further exacerbate inter-cell interference in dense macro deployments. This motivates the need for inter-cell interference coordination (ICIC) in multi-cell 5G NR networks serving both aerial and terrestrial users. In this work, we propose an ICIC framework that jointly optimizes antenna-domain coordination through BS uptilt angle optimization and time-domain interference coordination (TDIC) through NR-compliant scheduling. The framework is formulated as a multi-cell NR macro deployment problem that maximizes the minimum UAV signal-to-interference ratio (SIR) over a spatial grid of UAV locations while maintaining acceptable performance for ground user equipment (GUEs). The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and is solved using bio-inspired optimization techniques, including particle swarm optimization (PSO) and genetic algorithm (GA). Simulation results demonstrate that coordinated uptilt optimization with the booster-cell architecture significantly improves worst-case UAV SIR and downlink reliability in multi-cell 5G NR networks. booster-cell architecture significantly improves worst-case UAV SIR and downlink reliability in multi-cell 5G NR networks.
papers.summary_cn: 蜂窝连接无人机在5G NR宏网络中面临严重的下行非均匀干扰，主要源于基站下倾天线旁瓣。本文提出联合优化天线上倾角与时域干扰协调(NR调度)的ICIC框架，以最大化无人机最小SIR并保持地面用户性能。采用PSO和GA求解非凸优化问题，仿真表明协调上倾优化与增强小区架构显著提升多小区5G NR网络中无人机最差SIR和下行可靠性。

strengths: Addresses a relevant and timely problem: UAV connectivity in 5G NR networks is an emerging use case with practical industry relevance
Proposes a joint optimization framework combining antenna-domain (uptilt) and time-domain coordination, which is methodologically sound
Formulates a max-min SIR problem that explicitly balances aerial and terrestrial user performance, showing awareness of trade-offs
Uses bio-inspired optimization (PSO, GA) which is appropriate for non-convex problems, though not novel in itself
limitations: Abstract only mentions 'simulation results' with no indication of real-world datasets, field measurements, or even realistic 3GPP channel models
No mention of baseline comparisons against state-of-the-art ICIC schemes (e.g., CoMP, eICIC, FeICIC) or 3GPP aerial UAV study items
Bio-inspired optimization (PSO/GA) is computationally expensive and may not be practical for real-time deployment in live networks
Lacks discussion of NR-specific features that could be leveraged (e.g., SSB-based measurements, beam management, RRC reconfiguration delays)
No quantitative results provided in abstract (e.g., 'X dB SIR improvement') making impact assessment difficult
Unclear how 'booster-cell' differs from standard cell range extension or antenna retuning
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Major concern: Abstract indicates pure simulation-based evaluation with no mention of real datasets, 3GPP-compliant channel models, or validation against field measurements. The term 'simulation results' without further qualification typically implies synthetic data. No comparison against established baselines mentioned.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This is a solid but incremental contribution to aerial UAV communications in 5G. The problem is real and the joint optimization approach is reasonable, but the methodology relies on well-known heuristic techniques without clear validation against real-world data or established baselines. The lack of quantitative results and comparison details in the abstract limits confidence in the claims. Likely a mid-tier conference or journal paper (e.g., IEEE VTC, ICC workshops) rather than a top-tier venue.

overflow_list.title: A Case Study on Energy-Efficient Edge AI Crack Segmentation
overflow_list.authors: Matthias Tschope, Mohamed Moursi, Vladimir Rybalkin, Bo Zhou, Norbert Wehn, Paul Lukowicz
overflow_list.abstract: Crack segmentation on edge devices can support continuous infrastructure monitoring and maintenance and thereby help to preserve public safety. Furthermore, autonomous infrastructure monitoring by using Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) can reduce inspection risks, as human operators no longer need to enter hazardous areas. Edge processing reduces the cost of inspection by eliminating the need for high resolution image storage for offline processing and mitigates the security risks and bandwidth requirements of streaming to cloud servers. Edge inference is difficult due to the limited memory and computational capabilities of edge devices, which can affect both accuracy and latency. Furthermore, battery-powered devices are subject to strict power and energy constraints. Together, these limitations impose restrictions on the model size and computational complexity that can be deployed close to the sensor. In recent years, Transformers have achieved state-of-the-art accuracy in a variety of applications, including semantic segmentation. However, Transformer-based models are typically large and computationally intensive, making efficient edge deployment difficult. To address this, we first apply knowledge distillation to enhance the performance of the base models. We then use PTQ to compress the models further. Additionally, we consider the deployment of these models across multiple edge platforms. To maximize energy efficiency, we design and implement a custom hardware architecture for the models on an FPGA. Our results show that Knowledge Distillation (KD) improves all tested U-Net variants. Among the evaluated platforms, the selected FPGA implementation achieves 398 FPS at 204.99 Frames/J while maintaining a mean IoU of 69.42%. In addition, our best model reaches 71.92% mean IoU, which is 8.82 percentage points (pps) higher than the previously reported result on the CrackVision12K dataset.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 边缘设备上的裂缝分割可支持连续的基础设施监测与维护，从而有助于保障公共安全。此外，利用无人机进行自主基础设施监测可降低巡检风险，因为操作人员无需进入危险区域。边缘处理通过消除高分辨率图像离线存储需求来降低巡检成本，并规避了向云端服务器传输带来的安全风险和带宽要求。边缘推理受限于设备有限的内存和计算能力，这可能影响准确率和延迟。此外，电池供电设备面临严格的功耗和能量限制。这些限制共同制约了可部署在传感器附近的模型规模和计算复杂度。近年来，Transformer在包括语义分割在内的多种应用中达到了最先进的准确率，但基于Transformer的模型通常规模大、计算密集，难以高效部署到边缘。为此，我们首先应用知识蒸馏提升基础模型性能，然后使用PTQ进一步压缩模型。此外，我们考虑在多个边缘平台上部署这些模型。为最大化能效，我们在FPGA上为模型设计并实现了定制硬件架构。结果表明，知识蒸馏(KD)改进了所有测试的U-Net变体。在评估的平台中，所选FPGA实现达到398 FPS和204.99 Frames/J的能效，同时保持69.42%的平均IoU。此外，我们的最佳模型达到71.92%的平均IoU，比CrackVision12K数据集上先前报告的结果高出8.82个百分点。

strengths: Addresses a practically important problem — crack segmentation on resource-constrained edge/UAV platforms for infrastructure monitoring
Multi-platform deployment evaluation (GPU, FPGA, etc.) provides useful cross-platform comparison
Knowledge distillation + PTQ + custom FPGA design is a reasonable and complete edge deployment pipeline
8.82 pps improvement over previous SOTA on CrackVision12K is a meaningful result
FPGA achieving 398 FPS at 204.99 Frames/J with 69.42% mIoU demonstrates strong energy-efficiency focus
limitations: Methodologically incremental — knowledge distillation and PTQ are well-established techniques, and applying them in sequence is a standard recipe rather than a novel contribution
Transformer-based models are mentioned but the core contribution seems to focus on U-Net variants, making the Transformer framing somewhat misleading
CrackVision12K is a single benchmark; generalization to other crack/defect datasets or real-world deployment scenarios is unclear
No comparison against recent lightweight segmentation architectures designed specifically for edge (e.g., MobileNet-based, PIDNet, Fast-SCNN)
The gap between best mIoU (71.92%) and the FPGA-deployed mIoU (69.42%) suggests a non-trivial accuracy-efficiency tradeoff that is not fully analyzed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Evaluation is limited to the CrackVision12K dataset, a single benchmark. While the 8.82 pps improvement over prior SOTA is notable, the absence of cross-dataset validation, real-world field testing, or domain-shift analysis (e.g., different materials, lighting, noise conditions) significantly weakens the data quality claim. No ablation on dataset diversity or robustness to deployment conditions is mentioned.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper tackles a well-motivated practical problem with a solid engineering pipeline, but the methodological contribution is incremental. Knowledge distillation and PTQ are standard compression techniques, and their sequential application lacks novelty. The single-dataset evaluation further limits the strength of claims. Strong energy-efficiency FPGA results provide practical value, but the overall contribution is more of an engineering case study than a research advance.

overflow_list.title: Look One Step Ahead: Forward-Looking Incentive Design with Strategic Privacy for Proactive Service Provisioning over Air-Ground Integrated Edge Networks
overflow_list.authors: Sicheng Wu, Minghui Liwang, Yangyang Gao, Deqing Wang, Wenbo Zhu, Yiguang Hong, Wei Ni, Seyyedali Hosseinalipour
overflow_list.abstract: In air-ground integrated networks (AGINs), unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) provide on-demand edge services to ground vehicles. Realizing this vision requires carefully designed incentives to coordinate interactions among self-interested participants. This is exacerbated by the dynamic nature of AGINs, where spatio-temporal variations introduce significant uncertainty in matching UAVs and vehicles. Existing real-time service provisioning typically relies on precise trajectory information, raising privacy concerns and incurring decision latency. To address these challenges, we propose look one-step ahead (LOSA), a novel framework for efficient and privacy-aware service provisioning. By exploiting predictable vehicle travel times between intersections, LOSA decomposes the process into two coupled phases: (i) a privacy-aware look-ahead phase and (ii) a lightweight real-time execution phase. The look-ahead phase allows vehicles to adaptively adjust privacy budgets based on historical utility, balancing trajectory exposure and matching accuracy. Leveraging this, a double auction mechanism establishes binding one-step-ahead agreements (OSAAs) through trajectory similarity clustering, while constructing preference lists to hedge against mobility uncertainty. The execution phase then enforces pre-established OSAAs and preference lists, resolving real-time resource conflicts without costly re-negotiations. This design reduces computational overhead and preserves robustness. We analytically corroborate that LOSA guarantees truthfulness, individual rationality, and budget balance. Experiments on real-world datasets (DAIR-V2X, HighD, and RCooper) demonstrate that LOSA achieves superior privacy protection while lowering transaction latency compared to baseline approaches.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 在空地一体化网络(AGIN)中，无人机为地面车辆提供按需边缘服务。实现这一愿景需要精心设计激励机制来协调自利参与者之间的交互。AGIN的动态特性加剧了这一问题，时空变化给无人机与车辆的匹配带来显著不确定性。现有实时服务供给通常依赖精确轨迹信息，引发隐私担忧并产生决策延迟。为应对这些挑战，我们提出"向前看一步"(LOSA)框架，实现高效且隐私感知的服务供给。通过利用车辆在路口间可预测的行驶时间，LOSA将过程分解为两个耦合阶段：(i)隐私感知的预判阶段和(ii)轻量级实时执行阶段。预判阶段允许车辆基于历史效用自适应调整隐私预算，平衡轨迹暴露与匹配精度。在此基础上，双重拍卖机制通过轨迹相似性聚类建立具有约束力的一步预判协议(OSAA)，同时构建偏好列表以对冲移动不确定性。执行阶段则执行预先建立的OSAA和偏好列表，无需代价高昂的重新协商即可解决实时资源冲突。该设计降低了计算开销并保持鲁棒性。理论分析证实LOSA保证真实性、个体理性和预算平衡。在真实数据集(DAIR-V2X、HighD和RCooper)上的实验表明，LOSA在实现更优隐私保护的同时，相比基线方法降低了交易延迟。

strengths: Novel problem formulation combining forward-looking incentive design with strategic privacy in AGINs
Two-phase decomposition (look-ahead + execution) effectively addresses latency and privacy trade-offs
Double auction with trajectory similarity clustering is a practical design choice
Theoretical guarantees (truthfulness, individual rationality, budget balance) are analytically sound
Uses three real-world datasets: DAIR-V2X, HighD, RCooper—all established benchmarks
Forward-looking agreements (OSAAs) reduce real-time renegotiation overhead
Privacy budget adaptation based on historical utility is an elegant mechanism design
limitations: Privacy budget adaptation mechanism lacks rigorous optimality proof—the adaptive scheme is heuristic
Trajectory similarity clustering parameters (e.g., similarity thresholds) appear tuned heuristically without sensitivity analysis
Assumes predictable travel times between intersections—this assumption may not hold in highly dynamic urban scenarios
Missing comparison with state-of-the-art privacy-preserving matching algorithms
No discussion of differential privacy (ε, δ) parameter selection methodology or its impact on matching accuracy
Preference list construction details are vague; how hedging against uncertainty works exactly is unclear
Limited scalability analysis for large-scale UAV-vehicle networks
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Uses three real datasets (DAIR-V2X, HighD, RCooper), which are legitimate benchmarks. However, no details on dataset scale (number of vehicles, UAVs,时空范围) or how they simulate the UAV provisioning scenario. The paper claims superior privacy protection—quantitative privacy-utility trade-off details would strengthen this.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Score sits at 68/100, reflecting a solid contribution with novel integration of privacy and incentives but moderate technical depth. The novelty score is boosted by the fresh problem angle, while rigor is capped due to heuristics in clustering and privacy budget adaptation. Data quality is decent (3 real datasets) but lacks scale/discussion clarity. Impact potential is significant if assumptions hold in practice, which the community will test.

overflow_list.title: Early Exiting U-Net for Efficient Processing on UAVs: A Case Study in Environmental Monitoring
overflow_list.authors: Luca Sartori Boni, Mohamed Moursi, Norbert Wehn, Bilal Hammoud
overflow_list.abstract: Oil spills represent a severe threat, making early-stage thickness estimation crucial for guiding remediation efforts. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are an attractive platform for environmental monitoring. However, due to their limited computation and power budgets, real-time onboard processing requires optimized algorithms or lightweight machine learning models. While the standard U-Net architecture is often too large for constrained UAV hardware, the compressed Tiny U-Net variant fits on FPGA platforms and achieves competitive estimation performance (0.79 in the metric Intersection over Union, or IoU). Despite this success, Tiny U-Net processes every radar image through the complete inference pipeline, resulting in unnecessary computation for simple cases. To address this inefficiency, we integrate an early exit feature into the Tiny U-Net architecture. We introduce an early exit branch that returns an early prediction when a compact confidence score exceeds a tunable threshold, bypassing deeper layers for high-confidence evaluations. Our experiments demonstrate that this design achieves comparable IoU to the full baseline model. Crucially, the technique is shown to reduce the average number of multiplications by up to 42% for an aggressive threshold, reducing the dynamic power consumption. Choosing a threshold that ensures extreme confidence reduces the complexity-reduction gains for an improved IoU. This early exit approach substantially improves computational efficiency in Tiny U-Net, enabling more practical deployment in UAV-based environmental monitoring systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 溢油构成严重威胁，早期厚度估算对指导修复工作至关重要。无人机是环境监测的理想平台，但受限于计算和功耗预算，实时机载处理需要优化算法或轻量级机器学习模型。标准U-Net架构对受限无人机硬件通常过大，而压缩版Tiny U-Net变体可适配FPGA平台并实现具有竞争力的估算性能（IoU指标0.79）。尽管取得成功，Tiny U-Net仍对每个雷达图像执行完整推理流程，导致简单案例产生不必要计算。为解决这一低效问题，我们将早期退出特性集成到Tiny U-Net架构中。引入早期退出分支，当紧凑置信度分数超过可调阈值时返回早期预测，绕过深层网络处理高置信度评估。实验表明该设计达到与完整基线模型相当的IoU。关键的是，该技术在激进阈值下可减少高达42%的平均乘法运算次数，降低动态功耗。选择确保极高置信度的阈值会在改善IoU的同时降低复杂度削减收益。这种早期退出方法显著提升了Tiny U-Net的计算效率，使其在基于无人机的环境监测系统中更具实用部署价值。

strengths: Practical and well-motivated problem: UAV compute constraints for real-time environmental monitoring are real and significant
Up to 42% reduction in multiplications with early exiting is a meaningful efficiency gain for FPGA deployment
Tunable threshold provides a useful IoU-complexity trade-off knob for deployment scenarios
Clear and well-structured abstract that communicates problem, approach, and results effectively
limitations: Early exiting is a well-established technique (BranchyNet, 2016); applying it to Tiny U-Net is incremental novelty at best
Abstract says 'comparable IoU' without providing the actual early-exit IoU number — evasive framing raises credibility concerns
No information about the dataset: real vs. simulated radar imagery, sample size, or data collection methodology — critical gap
'Compact confidence score' is undefined; no mathematical formulation of the exit criterion is provided
'Aggressive threshold' yielding 42% reduction is vague — readers cannot reproduce or assess the trade-off
Narrow application scope (oil spill thickness estimation) limits generalizability claims
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The abstract provides no dataset details whatsoever — source, scale, real vs. synthetic, train/test split, or baseline comprehensiveness. The baseline IoU of 0.79 is stated but the early-exit variant's IoU is only described as 'comparable,' which is suspiciously imprecise. Without transparency on data provenance and size, the empirical claims cannot be properly assessed.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This is a straightforward application of early exiting (a known technique) to a compressed U-Net variant. The efficiency gains are plausible but unsurprising. The core concern is the lack of dataset transparency and the vague reporting of the early-exit IoU, which suggests the accuracy-complexity trade-off may be less favorable than implied. The paper addresses a real deployment need but offers limited technical novelty or empirical depth.

overflow_list.title: A Variational Message Passing Framework for Multi-Sensor Multi-Object Tracking using Raw Radar Signals
overflow_list.authors: Anders Malthe Westerkam, Jakob Möderl, Erik Leitinger, Troels Pedersen
overflow_list.abstract: The growing proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) poses major challenges for reliable airspace surveillance, as drones are typically small, have low radar cross-sections, and often move slowly in cluttered environments. These characteristics make the joint tasks of detecting, localizing, and tracking multiple objects difficult for conventional detect-then-track (DTT) approaches, which rely on pre-processed measurements and may discard informative low-signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) signal components. To overcome these limitations, we propose a variational message passing (VMP)-based direct multiobject tracking (MOT) method that operates directly on raw radar signals and explicitly accounts for an unknown and time-varying number of objects. The proposed method is formulated for MIMO multi-radar systems and performs data fusion by jointly processing the signals of all radar sensors using a probabilistic model. A superimposed signal model is employed to capture correlations in the raw sensor data caused by closely spaced objects, and a hierarchical Bernoulli-Gamma model is introduced to jointly model object existence, reflectivities, and the reliability of individual radar-object links. Using a mean-field approximation, we derive message updates, yielding a computationally efficient VMP algorithm that simultaneously performs object detection, track formation, state estimation, and nuisance parameter learning directly from the radar signal. Simulation results in synthetic scenarios with weak and closely-spaced objects show that the proposed direct-MOT method outperforms a conventional pipeline based on super-resolution estimation followed by belief propagation (BP)-based tracking, particularly in low-SNR and clutter-rich conditions, demonstrating the advantages of direct signal-level inference and coherent multi-radar fusion.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 针对无人机（UAV）监测难题，提出基于变分消息传递的直接多目标跟踪方法，可直接处理雷达原始信号，实现未知时变目标数的联合检测定位跟踪，在低信噪比和杂波环境下优于传统检测后跟跟踪方案。

strengths: End-to-end probabilistic formulation from raw radar signals avoids information loss inherent in detect-then-track pipelines, which is a genuinely meaningful architectural choice for low-SNR UAV detection

The hierarchical Bernoulli-Gamma model jointly capturing object existence, reflectivity, and radar-object link reliability is a well-motivated design for handling unknown object counts and unreliable per-sensor observations
Coherent multi-radar fusion via superimposed signal model rather than post-hoc track fusion is a principled approach that better exploits spatial diversity in MIMO systems
Mean-field VMP derivation yields computational tractability — important for real-time radar processing constraints
limitations: Evaluation is entirely on synthetic data with no real-world radar dataset validation, which severely limits confidence in practical deployability and robustness to real-world noise, interference, and hardware imperfections
No comparison against state-of-the-art deep learning-based tracking methods (e.g., radar CNNs, transformer-based MOT), only against a conventional BP-based pipeline — the baseline set appears narrow
Mean-field approximation quality in dense multi-object scenarios with strong signal correlations is unaddressed — this is where the independence assumption degrades most
Scalability to large numbers of objects and high-dimensional state spaces is not discussed; VMP with mean-field may face challenges as the object count grows
No discussion of computational complexity scaling or real-time feasibility on actual radar hardware
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Synthetic-only evaluation is the critical weakness. For a paper claiming practical advantages over DTT pipelines in realistic conditions, validation on at least one public radar dataset (e.g., uRAD, CRUW, or automotive radar benchmarks) or controlled experimental data would be essential. Synthetic data allows tuning favorable conditions and cannot demonstrate robustness to real-world artifacts such as clutter non-stationarity, multipath, and sensor calibration errors.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The theoretical framework is sound and the raw-signal-level inference direction is well-motivated for the UAV detection problem. However, synthetic-only validation, narrow baselines, and unaddressed scalability concerns limit the score. A mid-tier venue acceptance is likely if experiments are expanded; for top venues (IEEE TSP, TSP, or ICASSP), real-data validation would be expected.

overflow_list.title: Toward Environment-Aware LAE: SAR as a Shared Sensing Infrastructure
overflow_list.authors: Xue Zhang, Bang Huang, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
overflow_list.abstract: The rapid growth of the low-altitude economy (LAE) is making aerial systems an important part of future digital infrastructure. Although major advances have been achieved in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) platforms, communications, and autonomous control, environmental perception remains a key bottleneck to reliable and scalable LAE operations. Existing sensing modalities, such as optical, LiDAR, and millimeter-wave radar, are limited by visibility, sensing range, and environmental conditions, resulting in fragmented situational awareness. This article argues that addressing these limitations requires a shift from platform-centric sensing to a shared, environment-aware sensing infrastructure. In this context, synthetic aperture radar (SAR) offers a distinct advantage by enabling all-weather, wide-area perception. We show that SAR can support UAV operations through global environmental awareness, enhance task-level sensing, and enable cooperative sensing across satellites, high-altitude platforms, UAVs, and ground systems. Building on this perspective, we present a system-level view of SAR-enabled LAE, highlighting key transformations from fragmented to infrastructure-centric sensing, from reactive to predictive operation, and from device-centric to environment-aware networking. We further discuss enabling architectures, including multi-platform sensing hierarchies, integration with integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), and the role of artificial intelligence and digital twins, along with the key challenges toward real-world deployment. By positioning SAR as a shared sensing foundation rather than a standalone modality, this article provides new insights into the design of scalable, reliable, and intelligent LAE systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 低空经济(LAE)的快速增长正使空中系统成为未来数字基础设施的重要组成部分。尽管在无人机平台、通信和自主控制方面取得重大进展，环境感知仍是制约可靠可扩展LAE运营的关键瓶颈。现有感知模态（光学、激光雷达、毫米波雷达）受限于能见度、感知范围和环境条件，导致态势感知碎片化。本文认为，解决这些局限需要从平台中心感知转向共享的环境感知基础设施。在此背景下，合成孔径雷达(SAR)具有独特优势，可实现全天候广域感知。我们展示SAR可通过全局环境感知支持无人机运营、增强任务级感知，并实现卫星、高空平台、无人机和地面系统间的协同感知。基于这一视角，我们呈现SAR赋能LAE的系统级视图，强调从碎片化到基础设施中心化感知、从反应式到预测式运营、从设备中心到环境感知网络的关键转变。我们进一步讨论使能架构，包括多平台感知层级、与通感一体化(ISAC)的集成，以及人工智能和数字孪生的作用，还有实际部署面临的关键挑战。通过将SAR定位为共享感知基础而非独立模态，本文为可扩展、可靠、智能的LAE系统设计提供新见解。

strengths: Novel concept of SAR as shared sensing infrastructure for LAE
Clear system-level framework with three key transformations
Addresses real bottleneck (environmental perception) in UAV operations
Well-structured abstract with logical flow
Discusses relevant enabling technologies (ISAC, AI, digital twins)
Multi-platform sensing hierarchy architecture is insightful
limitations: No real data or experimental validation presented - appears to be a perspective/viewpoint article
No mathematical derivations or rigorous methodology
Lacks quantitative analysis or simulation results
No comparison with existing sensing modalities
No discussion of specific SAR systems or frequency bands
Unclear technical feasibility of proposed architectures
Missing discussion of latency requirements for UAV operations
No mention of cost, regulatory, or spectrum allocation challenges
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: This is a conceptual perspective article. The abstract shows no evidence of real-world dataset evaluation, simulations with standard benchmarks, or comparison with state-of-the-art methods. No datasets, experiments, or quantitative results are mentioned - likely a vision/roadmap paper for IEEE Communications Magazine or similar venue.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Score reflects that this is a visionary perspective piece (not a research paper with technical contributions). The concept is interesting but lacks rigor and data. High presentation score for well-written abstract, but low data/rigor scores as no methodological or experimental validation is described.

overflow_list.title: GreenScatter: Through-Canopy Soil Moisture Sensing with UAV-Mounted Radar
overflow_list.authors: Luke Jacobs, Ishfaq Aziz, Benhao Lu, Alireza Tabatabaeenejad, Mohamad Alipour, Elahe Soltanaghai
overflow_list.abstract: Soil moisture is a critical variable for managing irrigation, improving crop yield, and understanding field-scale hydrology. Radars mounted on unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) offer a promising means to monitor soil moisture over large fields with flexible, high-resolution coverage. However, during the growing season, canopy scattering and soil reflections become strongly coupled in the radar measurement. These coupled effects vary with crop structure or flight altitude, complicating the retrieval of soil moisture. To overcome this challenge, we present GreenScatter, a physics-based soil moisture retrieval framework for nadir-looking wideband UAV radars. GreenScatter introduces a microwave radiative transfer model that explicitly captures the dominant electromagnetic interactions between vegetation and soil, enabling accurate modeling of coherent ground backscatter through canopy. In parallel, it develops a radar cross-section (RCS) estimation method that transforms time-domain radar signals into calibrated wideband RCS spectra, isolating soil reflections while compensating for hardware and waveform effects. Together, these components enable robust soil moisture estimation through vegetation across varying canopy conditions and UAV configurations. Field experiments across multiple corn and soybean sites demonstrate consistent retrieval with an average volumetric water content (VWC) error of 4.49%.
overflow_list.summary_cn: GreenScatter：一种面向无人机宽带雷达的物理层土壤水分反演框架。通过建立植被-土壤电磁辐射传输模型结合时域雷达截面定标，实现穿透冠层获取土壤介电常数，在多地玉米大豆田间实验中平均体积含水量误差为4.49%。

strengths: Addresses a practically important and technically hard problem — decoupling canopy scattering from soil reflection in UAV radar measurements
Physics-based radiative transfer model that explicitly models vegetation-soil EM coupling is a principled approach vs. pure empirical inversion
RCS estimation method that compensates for hardware and waveform effects adds engineering credibility
Real field experiments across multiple crop types (corn and soybean), not just simulations
4.49% VWC error is a concrete and meaningful result for precision agriculture applications
limitations: Abstract claims 'multiple' corn and soybean sites but does not specify number of sites, field size, or sample count — scale may be limited
No mention of validation against other crop types beyond corn and soybean — generalizability to other canopies (wheat, rice, orchards) is unclear
Radiative transfer model novelty relative to existing vegetation scattering models (e.g., MIMICS, Tor Vergata) is not differentiated in the abstract
4.49% average VWC error could mask high variance across conditions; no mention of error distribution or worst-case performance
Unclear whether the framework handles extreme canopy densities or late-growth-stage crops where signal penetration may be negligible
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Uses real field experiment data across multiple corn and soybean sites, which is a genuine strength over simulation-only work. However, 'multiple sites' is vague — could be as few as 2-3. No information on temporal coverage, number of measurement dates, ground truth methodology, or comparison with state-of-the-art baselines. The single headline metric (4.49% VWC error) lacks context on distribution and conditions.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper tackles a real and technically challenging problem with a principled physics-based approach and real field validation, earning solid scores in rigor, data, and impact. Novelty is moderate — the components (radiative transfer modeling, RCS calibration) have precedents in the literature, but their integration for UAV through-canopy soil moisture retrieval is a meaningful contribution. The main drag on scores is the abstract's vagueness about dataset scale, baseline comparisons, and generalizability beyond two crop types.

overflow_list.title: Plasticity-Enhanced Multi-Agent Mixture of Experts for Dynamic Objective Adaptation in UAVs-Assisted Emergency Communication Networks
overflow_list.authors: Wen Qiu, Zhiqiang He, Wei Zhao, Hiroshi Masui
overflow_list.abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles serving as aerial base stations can rapidly restore connectivity after disasters, yet abrupt changes in user mobility and traffic demands shift the quality of service trade-offs and induce strong non-stationarity. Deep reinforcement learning policies suffer from plasticity loss under such shifts, as representation collapse and neuron dormancy impair adaptation. We propose plasticity enhanced multi-agent mixture of experts (PE-MAMoE), a centralized training with decentralized execution framework built on multi-agent proximal policy optimization. PE-MAMoE equips each UAV with a sparsely gated mixture of experts actor whose router selects a single specialist per step. A non-parametric Phase Controller injects brief, expert-only stochastic perturbations after phase switches, resets the action log-standard-deviation, anneals entropy and learning rate, and schedules the router temperature, all to re-plasticize the policy without destabilizing safe behaviors. We derive a dynamic regret bound showing the tracking error scales with both environment variation and cumulative noise energy. In a phase-driven simulator with mobile users and 3GPP-style channels, PE-MAMoE improves normalized interquartile mean return by 26.3\% over the best baseline, increases served-user capacity by 12.8\%, and reduces collisions by approximately 75\%. Diagnostics confirm persistently higher expert feature rank and periodic dormant-neuron recovery at regime switches.
overflow_list.summary_cn: PE-MAMoE：无人机基站灾后应急组网的多智能体强化学习框架。基于稀疏门控专家混合架构结合非参数相位控制器，在用户移动性和流量需求突变时重塑策略可塑性，实验显示归一化四分位平均收益提升26.3%，用户接入容量增加12.8%，碰撞率降低约75%。

strengths: Addresses a practically important problem: UAV-assisted emergency communication under non-stationary conditions
Well-motivated plasticity loss diagnosis (representation collapse, neuron dormancy) in multi-agent RL
Phase Controller is a principled, non-parametric approach to re-plasticization without learned parameters
Dynamic regret bound provides theoretical grounding linking tracking error to environment variation and noise energy
Strong empirical improvements: 26.3% interquartile mean return, 12.8% capacity gain, ~75% collision reduction
Diagnostic evidence (expert feature rank, dormant-neuron recovery) goes beyond task-level metrics
limitations: Core contribution is compositional: MoE + MAPPO + plasticity techniques are individually well-established; the combination is incremental rather than paradigm-shifting
Evaluation is entirely simulation-based (phase-driven simulator with 3GPP-style channels); no real UAV deployment or trace-driven validation
3GPP-style channel model is a simplification; real disaster environments involve complex propagation (debris, foliage, indoor penetration) not captured by standard models
Centralized training assumption may be impractical for true disaster scenarios where ground infrastructure is damaged
Abstract does not clarify whether phase switches are known a priori to the Phase Controller; if so, this is a strong oracle assumption that undermines practical applicability
No comparison with online learning or meta-RL baselines that are natural alternatives for non-stationary settings
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: All results come from a custom phase-driven simulator with synthetic mobile users and 3GPP-style channels. No real-world datasets, no trace-driven evaluation, and no hardware-in-the-loop testing. The simulator design appears reasonable but the absence of any real-data validation significantly weakens the claims, especially for a domain (disaster communication) where real-world conditions are notoriously hard to simulate accurately. The '3GPP-style' qualifier suggests simplified channel modeling rather than full 3GPP compliance.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper makes a solid incremental contribution by combining MoE with plasticity-aware training for multi-agent UAV optimization, backed by a regret bound and strong simulation results. However, the novelty is compositional rather than foundational, and the reliance on pure simulation with no real-world data is a major weakness—especially critical for a domain where real deployment conditions are highly uncertain. The theoretical result is a plus but the practical gap from simulation to deployment remains large.

overflow_list.title: Generative AI Agent Empowered Power Allocation for HAP Propulsion and Communication Systems
overflow_list.authors: Xiaoyu Xing, Dingyi Lu, Peng Yang, Zehui Xiong, Xianbin Cao, Tony Q. S. Quek
overflow_list.abstract: High altitude platforms (HAPs) are emerging as a key enabler for 6G coverage, yet limited energy must be split between propulsion and communications. Most prior HAP studies ignore propulsion power or rely on surrogates that miss hull-propeller interference, leading to misestimated communication power budgets and degraded beamforming. More importantly, HAP power allocation is intrinsically a multi-system, multidisciplinary problem in which aerodynamics, propulsion-system efficiency, and communication-system performance (quality of service (QoS) and energy efficiency (EE)) are tightly coupled.To address these challenges, this paper designs an interactive generative artificial intelligence (AI)-empowered HAP power allocation agent.By interacting with the AI agent, we develop an accurate propulsion power consumption model that takes into account the aerodynamic interference between the HAP's hull and the propeller. Assisted by the AI agent, we further formulate a HAP beamforming problem to improve user QoS and enhance the EE of the HAP communication system.This paper also proposes a QoS-enhanced energy-efficient (Q3E) beamforming algorithm to solve the formulated problem.Simulation results demonstrate the accuracy of the propulsion-power model and the effectiveness of the Q3E algorithm.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 针对6G高空平台（HAP）推进与通信能耗耦合问题，借助生成式AI构建精确推进功耗模型（考虑机身-螺旋桨干扰），提出Q3E波束赋形算法提升QoS和能效。

strengths: Addresses a real and underexplored problem: the propulsion-communication power coupling in HAPs, which most prior work ignores or oversimplifies
Propulsion power model incorporating hull-propeller aerodynamic interference is a physically meaningful improvement over surrogate-based approaches
Joint consideration of QoS and EE in beamforming formulation is practically relevant for HAP deployment
limitations: The 'generative AI agent' framing appears to be buzzword packaging — using an LLM to interactively develop models and formulate problems is not a genuine generative AI contribution; the core optimization (Q3E algorithm) appears to be traditional
Purely simulation-based evaluation with no real-world experimental validation or real dataset — critical for a paper claiming improved aerodynamic modeling accuracy
Abstract is vague on what the AI agent actually does algorithmically; unclear whether it contributes anything beyond problem formulation assistance
Beamforming for QoS+EE is well-trodden territory; the Q3E algorithm's novelty over existing energy-efficient beamforming methods is not established from the abstract
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Evaluation relies entirely on simulations. No real-world HAP measurement data, no experimental validation of the propulsion power model, and no comparison against real flight/power consumption data. This is a significant weakness for a paper whose central claim is a more accurate propulsion model.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The hull-propeller interference model is a solid incremental contribution, but the paper is weakened by (1) the 'generative AI agent' angle which seems more marketing than substance, (2) purely simulated validation for a paper claiming improved physical modeling accuracy, and (3) a beamforming formulation that adds limited novelty over the existing EE+QoS literature. Likely a mid-tier venue paper.

overflow_list.title: Quality-Aware Denoising of Ultra-Short TDoA Measurements for 5G-NR UAV Localization
overflow_list.authors: Zexin Fang, Bin Han, Anjie Qiu, Zhuojun Tian, Hans D. Schotten
overflow_list.abstract: Reliable positioning is essential for Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in safety-critical urban operations, yet achieving sub-meter accuracy under stringent latency constraints remains challenging. While 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) specifies repeated Positioning Reference Signals (PRS) transmissions for accurate Time Difference of Arrival (TDoA) measurements, denoising techniques specifically tailored for extremely limited measurement sequences within 3GPP frameworks remain underexplored. We propose Adaptive Gain Exponential Smoother (AGES), a lightweight filter combining exponentially weighted averaging with adaptive gains informed by 3GPP measurement quality reports. Simulations demonstrate AGES achieves 30-40% reduction in positioning error with only 3-5 repeated measurements while maintaining Fifth Generation New Radio (5G-NR) infrastructure compatibility.
overflow_list.summary_cn: AGES：面向5G-NR无人机定位的自适应增益指数平滑滤波器。针对3GPP定位参考信号极短测量序列设计，结合测量质量报告自适应调节增益，实现3-5次重复测量下定位误差降低30-40%，兼容5G-NR基础设施。

strengths: Well-motivated problem: sub-meter UAV localization under tight latency is practically important for urban safety-critical ops
Explicit integration with 3GPP measurement quality reports (e.g., RSRP/RSRQ-based gain) keeps the method standards-compliant
Ultra-short sequence regime (3-5 PRS repetitions) is a realistic and underexplored operating point compared to classical filtering that assumes many samples
